title: E-Waste Pathways to Oceanic Ecosystems
description: A flowchart illustrating how improperly managed electronic waste enters and impacts marine environments.
elements: Marine Pollutants (Heavy Metals, Microplastics, Flame Retardants)
Oceanic Ecosystem Components (Plankton, Fish, Coral Reefs, Marine Mammals)
relationships: Landfill leachate contaminates groundwater which flows into rivers and oceans
Runoff carries contaminants from recycling sites directly into water systems
Incineration releases particles into the air that settle on the ocean surface
Pollutants bioaccumulate in marine organisms and biomagnify up the food web
Toxins ultimately return to humans through the consumption of contaminated seafood
